column1: The table is organized roughly chronologically, using 456 BCE as a reference point
Figures before 456 BCE: King David, Orpheus, Linus, Musaeus, Tiresias, Homer, Hesiod, Vyasa, Valmiki, Zarathustra, Confucius, Solon, Epimenides, Laozi, Buddha
Figures after 456 BCE: Jesus Christ, Taliesin, Muhammad, Kabir, Rumi, Mirabai, Guru Nanak, Milarepa, Basavanna, Adi Shankaracharya, Hafez
Musical abilities: Many played instruments (David's harp, Orpheus' lyre)
Performance aspects: Public recitation or singing of works
Divine inspiration: Often claimed special connection to deities
Transmission of wisdom: Used poetic forms to teach spiritual truths
Mythic biography: Life stories often contain supernatural elements
Cultural preservation: Maintained historical and cultural identity
Philosophical insights: Conveyed complex ideas through accessible stories
Ritual functions: Works used in religious ceremonies and practices
